_comment: Reference lookup tables used by eligibility rules. Rules reference these tables by name for country-specific logic, certificate recognition, GPA normalization, and language thresholds. This file is the single source of truth for domain reference data.
_status_note: Contains representative sample data only. Real deployment requires verified data from Anabin, DAAD, and individual university admission offices.

english_native_countries._comment: Countries whose nationals are typically exempt from English language testing

recognized_certificates_by_country._comment: Maps countries to their secondary school certificates, Anabin default ratings, and whether a Studienkolleg/foundation year is typically required for direct university admission in Germany. Real data must be verified against current Anabin database.

US.notes: Direct admission with AP scores or SAT/ACT. Requirements vary by university.

GB.notes: A-Levels typically sufficient. GCSE alone is not.

JO.notes: Tawjihi holders typically require Studienkolleg for German public universities. Private universities may differ.

TR.notes: Studienkolleg required for most German public universities

IN.notes: Indian Class 12 typically requires Studienkolleg. Some universities accept with high scores.

NG.notes: Studienkolleg required. WAEC alone typically rated H- in Anabin.

CN.notes: Gaokao scores above provincial threshold may qualify for direct admission since 2020 APS reform.

gpa_normalization._comment: Grading system definitions used for cross-system GPA comparison. The engine uses these to normalize grades to a common scale when rules require it.

uk_classification.name: UK Degree Classification
uk_classification.note: Ordinal, not numeric — requires special comparison logic

studienkolleg_tracks._comment: German Studienkolleg preparation tracks. Each track maps to specific fields of study the student can pursue after completion.

language_test_validity._comment: How long language test results remain valid, in months from test date
